title: Pultenaea subalpina
taxonomy: Pultenaea subalpina is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Australia and is a part of the genus Pultenaea. The family Fabaceae is commonly known as the legume family due to the presence of fruit in the form of pods. Pultenaea subalpina is further classified into the order Fabales and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification system helps to group plants based on their shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
uses: Pultenaea subalpina is a plant native to Australia, and while specific information on its medicinal uses is limited, it's often used in traditional Australian medicine. intro: Pultenaea subalpina, also known as mountain bush-pea, is a shrub-like plant native to eastern Australia, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and dense foliage. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Pultenaea subalpina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pultenaea subalpina is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to relieve respiratory symptoms and soothe coughs.
